title: Dre Greenlaw Opens Up: San Francisco LB's Honest Admission About Tough 2025 Season with Broncos
body:
The San Francisco 49ers have welcomed back a familiar face in linebacker Dre Greenlaw, who has expressed his relief at returning to the team that originally drafted him. The 27-year-old veteran, who spent the 2025 season with the Denver Broncos, has revealed that a combination of factors contributed to a difficult year with the Mile High City team.

Greenlaw's time with the Broncos was marked by a series of setbacks, including injuries and limited playing opportunities. Despite being a key contributor to the 49ers' success in previous seasons, the linebacker struggled to find his footing in Denver, where he found himself splitting reps with other players. This lack of consistency and playing time ultimately took a toll on his performance, with Greenlaw admitting that he didn't play to his usual standards.

The 2025 season was a far cry from the form that Greenlaw had established with the 49ers, where he was a crucial member of the defense. During his time with San Francisco, the linebacker was a key factor in the team's success, known for his tackling ability and ability to read the opposing team's offense. However, in Denver, he found himself struggling to adapt to a new system and make an impact on the field.

Greenlaw's departure from the 49ers was seen as a surprise move by many, but it's clear in retrospect that the team was looking to shake up their defense. While the move may have been motivated by a desire to inject new talent and energy into the team, it ultimately proved to be a misstep. With Greenlaw now back in San Francisco, the 49ers will be hoping that he can recapture the form that made him such a valuable asset in the first place.

The news of Greenlaw's return will be a welcome boost to 49ers fans, who will be eager to see the linebacker back in action. With the team set to face a challenging schedule in the upcoming season, Greenlaw's experience and leadership will be crucial in helping the 49ers navigate the tough competition. As the team looks to build on their previous success, the return of Greenlaw is a significant development that could have a major impact on their fortunes.

While Greenlaw's time with the Broncos was undoubtedly a disappointment, it's clear that he's eager to put the past behind him and focus on the future. With the 49ers, he'll be reunited with a team that he knows and loves, and he'll be looking to make up for lost time in the upcoming season. As the 2026 campaign gets underway, fans will be watching closely to see if Greenlaw can recapture his former glory and help the 49ers make a serious push for the playoffs.
